notes: The canonical description standard: 'Max 1024 chars; third person; first sentence = what it does; second sentence = Use when [specific triggers].' Plus the split-files-at-100-lines rule and the review checklist. PRIMARY source for the skill-language-standard.

Token-compression drop-rules (~75% reduction): drop articles, filler, pleasantries, hedging; short synonyms; one word when one word enough. The auto-clarity exception (never compress security warnings / irreversible-action confirmations) is the safety boundary the body-tightening pass must respect. Source for the prose-economy half of the standard.
Forbidden-word discipline ('use these terms exactly; avoid X, say Y') and rejected-framings pattern. Source for the standard's terminology-consistency rule.

The sharpest prose-economy statement: 'No hedging, no throat-clearing, no it-is-worth-noting. If a sentence could be a bullet, make it a bullet. If a bullet could be cut, cut it.' Source for the standard's cut-rules.

MIT licence retained per its terms (attribution for substantial verbatim portions). The standard adopts ideas/patterns, not code — attribution here is hygiene, kept because verbatim files are co-located.
